"Irish" gives me Irish luck but nothing else. "common" works perfectly for common ground and middle post works in sports contexts, though neither captures all three meanings cleanly.

Let me explore "stadium"—it has posts and grounds, which could work, but feels risky. "burial ground" is solid, "trading post" is excellent, but these only hit one or two meanings each. I'm realizing I should probably play it safer and find words that clearly work for individual meanings: "fortune" for luck, "coffee" for ground, and "mail" for post are all straightforward options.

But reconsidering "break"—it connects strongly to both luck (lucky break) and ground (break ground/groundbreaking). The chair and nut associations exist but feel weaker than those primary connections, so I think "break" is actually the better choice here.
